name: Peñacorada (1832 m) – circular from Fuentes de Peñacorada
name_original: Peñacorada (1832 m) – circular desde Fuentes de Peñacorada
region_intro: Hiking around Cistierna offers access to diverse landscapes in the province of León, Spain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Picos de Europa, featuring majestic mountain scenery and sculpted valleys. Notable peaks like Peñacorada provide challenging climbs and panoramic vistas. The area also includes the Riaño Reservoir and varied flora, such as oak and beech forests, supporting a rich natural environment for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Cistierna The most popular hiking route is Route of the Mines – circular from Sabero (CR-6), a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate circular hike starts from Sabero. Another top favourite among local hikers is Miradores de Aquilare – circular through the Sabero Valley, a moderate 3.9 miles (6.2 km) path. This route offers scenic views as it winds through the Sabero Valley. Local hikers also love the Peñacorada (1832 m) – circular from Fuentes de Peñacorada, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Cistierna is defined by its mountain peaks, proximity to the Picos de Europa, and diverse forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding mountain excursions. The routes in Cistierna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Cistierna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Cistierna?
A: Cistierna offers a diverse range of hiking trails suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy walks to more demanding mountain excursions. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Picos de Europa, featuring majestic mountain scenery, sculpted valleys, and varied forests including oak and beech.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking routes in Cistierna?
A: Yes, Cistierna has options for beginners. Out of over 30 trails, 3 are classified as easy, providing gentle walks suitable for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed experience. The majority of routes are moderate, offering a good balance of challenge and accessibility.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Cistierna area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the popular routes around Cistierna are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050029208/route-of-the-mines-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mines – circular from Sabero (CR-6)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050020786/miradores-de-aquilare-circular-through-the-sabero-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradores de Aquilare – circular through the Sabero Valley&lt;/a&gt;, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047833782/penacorada-1832-m-circular-from-fuentes-de-penacorada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñacorada (1832 m) – circular from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on a hike in Cistierna?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter impressive peaks like Pico Gilbo and Peñacorada, offering panoramic vistas. The nearby Riaño Reservoir provides stunning views, and the area features diverse forests. Highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho&lt;/a&gt; are popular points of interest.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, Cistierna has a rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050029208/route-of-the-mines-circular-from-sabero-cr-6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mines&lt;/a&gt;, starting from Sabero, offers insights into the region's mining heritage. Additionally, the historic Camino Olvidado (Forgotten Way), a pilgrimage route to Santiago de Compostela, passes through Cistierna, blending natural beauty with cultural heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cistierna?
A: Cistierna's diverse landscapes make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with deciduous trees changing colors. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while winter can provide opportunities for snowshoeing or quieter walks, though some mountain routes may require specific gear and experience.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Cistierna suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, Cistierna offers family-friendly options, especially among its easier and moderate trails. The varied terrain and natural features, such as forests and viewpoints, can make for engaging walks for children. Always check the specific route's length and elevation gain to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Cistierna?
A: The varied climate and topography of Cistierna support abundant wildlife. Hikers might spot deer, roe deer, wild boars, squirrels, and various bird species. The Esla River is also known for its trout. Keep an eye out for tracks and signs of animals, especially in quieter forest areas.

Q: How do other hikers rate the trails in Cistierna?
A: The hiking routes in Cistierna are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the diverse natural environment, and the variety of trails available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific geological formations or caves to explore?
A: Yes, Cistierna offers opportunities for speleology. The Cueva del Carrascal is a notable cave in the area. For those seeking more adventure, there's also a via ferrata known as Cueva del Elefante, providing an exciting vertical experience amidst unique geological formations.

Q: What are some of the more challenging hikes in the Cistierna region?
A: For experienced hikers, Cistierna offers challenging mountain excursions. Peaks like Pico Gilbo (1,679 meters) and Peñacorada provide demanding climbs and significant elevation gains. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047833782/penacorada-1832-m-circular-from-fuentes-de-penacorada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñacorada (1832 m) – circular from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt; require good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Is Cistierna a good base for other outdoor activities besides hiking?
A: Absolutely. Beyond hiking, Cistierna is a hub for various outdoor pursuits. You can enjoy cycling, with routes like the 'Pedales de León' offering challenging adventures. The region also provides options for canyoning, via ferrata, and climbing, with equipped routes for different skill levels. The Esla River is popular for fishing.
tips: With an easy ascent if we are in good physical shape, we will reach the top of Peñacorada located at 1832 meters above sea level, which awaits us with fantastic views of the mountains of Leon and the Picos de Europa in the distance.

At this point we are in the Collado de la Bodeguina located at 1470 meters above sea level and with phenomenal views.
If we go down from Peñacorada we must know that the descent is technical and with sections of loose stone, so it is worth taking it easy.
Rolling along a wide forest track, we find these beautiful views of the town of Fuentes de Peñacorada.
